26.reason: [Error: Assignment operator '=' used instead of comparison operator '=='. This assigns 1 to num1 and always evaluates to true, causing incorrect logic.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Assignment operator '=' used instead of comparison operator '=='. This assigns 1 to num1 and always evaluates to true, causing incorrect logic.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Assignment operator '=' used instead of comparison operator '=='. This assigns 1 to num1 and always evaluates to true, causing incorrect logic.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Assignment (=) used instead of comparison (==); always true when s[0] != '0' but also changes num1 to 1.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Assignment '=' used instead of comparison '=='; this condition is always true when num1 is 1, but intended to check equality.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The expression `num1=1` uses the assignment operator instead of the equality comparison operator `==`. This will always evaluate to true and incorrectly assign 1 to num1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Assignment operator = used instead of equality operator ==, causing logical error and potential compilation error with strict warning flags]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Assignment '=' used instead of comparison '=='. This causes num1 to be set to 1 and condition always true if s[0]!='0', leading to incorrect handling of numbers like "0.123" or misclassification of single-digit non-zero cases.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Assignment operator '=' used instead of comparison operator '==', causing logical error and unintended assignment]

19.reason: Loop bound should be i<len, not i<len-1, because the last digit after the decimal point is omitted.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop bound should be i<len to include the last digit after decimal point.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Loop bound should be i<len, not i<len-1, because the last digit after the decimal point is omitted.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition `i<len-1` causes the last digit of the number to be omitted. It should be `i<len` to iterate through all remaining characters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition i<len-1 skips the last digit of the number; should be i<len]

52.reason: [Loop condition i!=len-1 stops before printing the last digit; should be i!=len or s[i]!='\0']

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition i!=len-1 stops before printing the last digit; should be i!=len or s[i]!='\0']

45.reason: [This condition checks if the last character before '\0' is the first non-zero, but strlen includes all characters; s[len] is '\0', so s[len-1] is last digit. However, if input ends right after decimal (e.g., "0.0"), this may access out-of-bound or misbehave. But more critically, if the number is like "0.1", then i=2, s[2]='1', so i != len-1 (if len=3, len-1=2, so i==2 → i==len-1 is true). Then it prints "1e-1", which is correct. However, the real issue is in the loop above: if the string has no non-zero after zeros (like "0.000"), but problem states "all digits are significant" and "no trailing zeros", so such input won't occur. So this may not be the main bug. The main bug is the assignment in the else-if condition.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition checks if the last character before '\0' is the first non-zero, but strlen includes all characters; s[len] is '\0', so s[len-1] is last digit. However, if input ends right after decimal (e.g., "0.0"), this may access out-of-bound or misbehave. But more critically, if the number is like "0.1", then i=2, s[2]='1', so i != len-1 (if len=3, len-1=2, so i==2 → i==len-1 is true). Then it prints "1e-1", which is correct. However, the real issue is in the loop above: if the string has no non-zero after zeros (like "0.000"), but problem states "all digits are significant" and "no trailing zeros", so such input won't occur. So this may not be the main bug. The main bug is the assignment in the else-if condition.]
